actions-list.actionItem: The House has concluded all anticipated legislative business and has proceeded to Special Order speeches.
The House proceeded with further one minute speeches.
The House observed a moment of silence in honor of a Pennsylvania servicemember who died in the line of duty.
The House observed a moment of silence in honor of two Kentucky servicemembers who died in the line of duty.
Mr. Lawler asked unanimous consent that when the House adjourns today, it adjourn to meet at 9:00 a.m. on March 19. Agreed to without objection.

actions-list.header: On motion to suspend the rules and pass the resolution Failed by the Yeas and Nays: (2/3 required): 211
actions-list.actionItem: 207 (Roll no. 95).
Considered as unfinished business. H.J. Res. 139 — "Proposing an amendment to the Constitution of the United States requiring a balanced budget for the Federal Government."
Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.

actions-list.header: On passage Passed by the Yeas and Nays: 231
actions-list.actionItem: 186 (Roll no. 94).

Considered as unfinished business. H.R. 1958 — "To amend the Immigration and Nationality Act to clarify that aliens who have been convicted of defrauding the United States Government or the unlawful receipt of public benefits are inadmissible and deportable."
Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.

actions-list.header: On passage Passed by recorded vote: 215
actions-list.actionItem: 202 (Roll no. 93).

actions-list.header: On motion to recommit Failed by the Yeas and Nays: 206
actions-list.actionItem: 209 (Roll no. 92).

Considered as unfinished business. H.R. 556 — "To prohibit the Secretary of the Interior and the Secretary of Agriculture from prohibiting the use of lead ammunition or tackle on certain Federal land or water under the jurisdiction of the Secretary of the Interior and the Secretary of Agriculture, and for other purposes."

The Chair announced the unfinished business was the question on motion to recommit H.R. 556, passage of H.R. 556, if ordered, passage of H.R. 1958, and on motion to suspend the rules and pass H.J. Res. 139, which had been debated earlier and on which further proceedings had been postponed.
The House convened, returning from a recess continuing the legislative day of March 18.
The Speaker announced that the House do now recess. The next meeting is subject to the call of the Chair.

The House received a communication from the Honorable Mike Johnson, Speaker of the House of Representatives. The Speaker notified the Committee on the Judiciary that Representative Kevin Kiley's election to the Committee on the Judiciary has been automatically vacated pursuant to clause 5(b) of rule X, effective March 18, 2026.

The House received a communication from the Honorable Mike Johnson, Speaker of the House of Representatives. The Speaker notified the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure that Representative Kevin Kiley's election to the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure has been automatically vacated pursuant to clause 5(b) of rule X, effective March 18, 2026.

The House received a communication from The Honorable Mike Johnson, Speaker of the House of Representatives. The Speaker notified the Committee on Education and Workforce that Representative Kevin Kiley's election to the Committee on Education and Workforce has been automatically vacated pursuant to clause 5(b) of rule X, effective March 18, 2026.

The House received a communication from the Honorable Lisa McClain, Chairwoman, House Republican Conference. Pursuant to clause 5(b)(1) of Rule X, Chairwoman McClain notified the House that Representative Kevin Kiley has resigned as a member of the House Republican Conference.

At the conclusion of debate on H.R. 556, the Chair put the question on the motion to recommit and by voice vote, announced the noes had prevailed. Mrs. Dingell demanded the yeas and nays and the Chair postponed further proceedings until a time to be announced.
The previous question on the motion to recommit was ordered pursuant to clause 2(b) of rule XIX.
Mrs. Dingell moved to recommit to the Committee on Natural Resources.
The previous question was ordered pursuant to the rule.
The House proceeded with one hour of debate on H.R. 556.

Rule provides for consideration of H.R. 556, H.R. 1958 and H.R. 4638. The resolution provides for consideration of H.R. 556, H.R. 1958, and H.R. 4638 under a closed rule. The resolution provides for one hour of general debate and one motion to recommit on each bill.

Considered under the provisions of rule H. Res. 1115. H.R. 556 — "To prohibit the Secretary of the Interior and the Secretary of Agriculture from prohibiting the use of lead ammunition or tackle on certain Federal land or water under the jurisdiction of the Secretary of the Interior and the Secretary of Agriculture, and for other purposes."

At the conclusion of debate on H.R. 1958, the Chair put the question on passage of the bill and by voice vote, announced the ayes had prevailed. Mr. Raskin demanded the yeas and nays and the Chair postponed further proceedings until a time to be announced.
The previous question was ordered pursuant to the rule.
The House proceeded with one hour of debate on H.R. 1958.

Rule provides for consideration of H.R. 556, H.R. 1958 and H.R. 4638. The resolution provides for consideration of H.R. 556, H.R. 1958, and H.R. 4638 under a closed rule. The resolution provides for one hour of general debate and one motion to recommit on each bill.

Considered under the provisions of rule H. Res. 1115. H.R. 1958 — "To amend the Immigration and Nationality Act to clarify that aliens who have been convicted of defrauding the United States Government or the unlawful receipt of public benefits are inadmissible and deportable."

At the conclusion of debate, the Yeas and Nays were demanded and ordered. Pursuant to the provisions of clause 8, rule XX, the Chair announced that further proceedings on the motion would be postponed.
Pursuant to the provisions of H. Res. 1115, the House proceeded with one hour of debate on H.J. Res. 139
Considered under suspension of the rules.

Mr. Biggs (AZ) moved to suspend the rules and pass the resolution. H.J. Res. 139 — "Proposing an amendment to the Constitution of the United States requiring a balanced budget for the Federal Government."
The Speaker announced that votes on suspensions, if ordered, will be postponed until a time to be announced.
The House proceeded with one minute speeches which by direction of the Chair, would be limited to 15 per side of the aisle.
The Chair designated Mr. Harris of NC to lead the Members in reciting the Pledge of Allegiance to the Flag.
The Speaker announced approval of the Journal.  Pursuant to clause 1, rule I, the Journal stands approved.
Today's prayer was offered by the House Chaplain, Chaplain Margaret Grun Kibben.
The House convened, returning from a recess continuing the legislative day of March 18.
The Speaker announced that the House do now recess. The next meeting is scheduled for 12:00 P.M. today.
The House proceeded with Morning-Hour Debate. At the conclusion of Morning-Hour, the House will recess until 12:00 p.m. for the start of legislative business.
The House convened, starting a new legislative day.
